parentQuestion: A patient cannot smile, raise their eyebrows, or close their eye tightly on the right side. Which nerve is damaged?

scaffolds.q: Your face can show emotions like happiness, sadness, and surprise.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Facial expressions communicate emotions. Smiling shows happiness, frowning shows sadness, raised eyebrows show surprise. Muscles in your face create these expressions.

scaffolds.q: Smiling requires muscles to pull the corners of your mouth upward.
scaffolds.explain: Exactly! Small muscles around your mouth contract to pull your lips into a smile. These are called muscles of facial expression, and theyre all controlled by one nerve.

scaffolds.q: CN VII is the abbreviation for Cranial Nerve number ___.
scaffolds.explain: CN VII = Cranial Nerve Seven! In Roman numerals, VII = 7. The facial nerve is the seventh cranial nerve.

scaffolds.q: The facial nerve is named because it controls the face.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The facial nerve (CN VII) controls the muscles that move your face - smiling, frowning, blinking, raising eyebrows. Its named for what it does!

scaffolds.q: The right side and left side of your face are controlled by separate nerves.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! You have a facial nerve on each side. The right CN VII controls the right side of your face, and the left CN VII controls the left side.

scaffolds.q: If only one side of someones face is drooping, only one nerve is damaged.
scaffolds.explain: Right! Since each side has its own nerve, damage to just the right facial nerve causes problems only on the right side. The left side still works normally.

scaffolds.q: Closing your eyes tightly uses muscles around your eyes.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The orbicularis oculi muscle circles your eye and squeezes it shut. This muscle, like all facial expression muscles, is controlled by the facial nerve (CN VII).

scaffolds.q: Smiling, frowning, and raising eyebrows all use facial muscles.
scaffolds.explain: Facial expressions are produced by muscles of facial expression - small muscles that attach to the skin. Theyre all controlled by CN VII.

scaffolds.q: The same nerve controls all muscles of facial expression on one side.
scaffolds.explain: The facial nerve (CN VII) provides motor innervation to all muscles of facial expression on one side of the face. Lose it, and half your face stops moving.
